title: ParseContextWorkStateSurfaced payload
description: Emitted by the parse_context handler on every call that ran the work-state resolver (chain parse-context-lean-orienting T6). T10's measurement consumes the event for fire-rate (work-state-firing vs other intents), surface mix (which of bugs/tasks/chains land per call), and cache-hit-rate dashboards. The no-surfacings case (counts all zero) still emits so T10 has a fire-rate denominator.

intent_shape.description: The directive intent that fired this surfacing. Always one of the work-state firing set today (verify/implement/fix/audit/status/list); explain/summarize/none never appear here because the resolver short-circuits on those shapes. Kept as the full vocabulary so future expansion doesn't need a schema bump.

bugs_count.description: Number of open bug Candidates surfaced this call. Capped at 10 (workStateMaxBugs in resolver source).

tasks_count.description: Number of active task Candidates surfaced this call. Capped at 5.

chains_count.description: Number of recent open chain Candidates surfaced this call. Capped at 3, within the 7-day recency window.

cache_hit.description: True when the work-state resolver served the response from its short-5-turns cache instead of re-querying the DB. T10's cache-effectiveness dashboard reads this directly.

project_id.description: The project the resolver scoped to. Empty means the no-project short-circuit fired (constraint in T6 — no surfacings without a resolved project).
